question: Wimbledon ATP: Grigor Dimitrov vs Arthur Fery
description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Grigor Dimitrov and Arthur Fery in the Wimbledon ATP, originally scheduled for July 6, 2026 at 6:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Grigor Dimitrov' if Grigor Dimitrov advances against Arthur Fery.

This market will resolve to 'Arthur Fery' if Arthur Fery advances against Grigor Dimitrov.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Grigor Dimitrov enters this Wimbledon fourth-round matchup with strong momentum after defeating 15th seed Jakub Mensik in three sets, showcasing improved movement and grass-court efficiency following a year of pectoral injury setbacks that included a 2025 retirement against Jannik Sinner. The 35-year-old Bulgarian's all-court game and prior deep runs at the All England Club position him as the clear favorite against Arthur Fery. The 23-year-old British wildcard has posted a career-high ranking near No. 114 and advanced to the fourth round of a major for the first time with gritty five-set and four-set victories, buoyed by home support and recent grass form. Fery's serve-volley potential and crowd energy create realistic upset pathways on the surface, though experience gaps and Dimitrov's superior recent results shape trader consensus around a Dimitrov advance.
